scope: TG-P7: final default flip + strict census pass (packaging/gating phase)

blocker: decode_attention_owned_two_kernel is external_handwritten_kernel and the only generated replacement (G5-8B) is slower (TG-P5 refute). Per the stop rule, owned HIP stays the 8B attention default -> TINYGRAD_DEFAULT_PURITY_PASS is not reachable without a decode regression.

gates.strict_final_default: FAIL by design (exit 2: 1 external_handwritten default remains = decode_attention_owned_two_kernel)

gates.wd_protected: PASS on converted routes (Q4_K/Q6_K/prefill no regression); attention refuted (no regression forced)

outcome: 4 of 5 hot default routes are now machine_authored_generated and BoltBeam-selectable, with handwritten oracles one rollback flag away. The 5th (8B decode attention) is honestly blocked on speed. This is the correct terminal state given TG-P5; the north-star PASS would require a faster generated attention route (a future capability, not a packaging step).
